title: Query using Pre-Defined Replica Set Tags

sections.heading: Pre-Defined Replica Set Tag Descriptions
sections.body:
The following table describes the pre-defined replica set tags
that Atlas provides.

| Pre-Defined Tag Name | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Example                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Availability Zone    | AWS (Amazon Web Services) availability zone ID, Google Cloud fully-qualified name for a zone, or Azure (Microsoft Azure) zone number.Azure (Microsoft Azure) supports availability zones only in a subset of regions. Atlas provides pre-defined availability zone tags for Azure (Microsoft Azure) only for regions that support availability zones. To learn more, see Microsoft Azure.For more information about the possible availabilityZone values for each cloud provider, see the cloud provider's documentation:AWSGCPAzure | AWS (Amazon Web Services): \{"availabilityZone" : "us-east-1a"}Google Cloud: \{"availabilityZone" : "us-east1-b"}Azure (Microsoft Azure): \{"availabilityZone" : "1"} |
| Node Type            | Node type.Possible values are:ELECTABLEREAD\_ONLYANALYTICSFor more information, see Node Types.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | \{"nodeType" : "ANALYTICS"}                                                                                                                                           |
| Provider             | Cloud provider on which the node is provisioned.Possible values are:AWSGCPAZURE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | \{"provider" : "AWS"}                                                                                                                                                 |
| Region               | Cloud region in which the node resides.For a complete list of possible region values, see the reference page for your cloud provider:AWSGCPAzure                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | \{"region" : "US\_EAST\_2"}                                                                                                                                           |
| Workload Type        | Pre-defined replica set tag to distribute your workload evenly among your non-analytics (electable or read-only) nodes.Possible values are:OPERATIONAL                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | \{"workloadType" : "OPERATIONAL"}                                                                                                                                     |
| Disk State           | State of your disk.Possible value: READYFor more information, see Disk States.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | \{"diskState" : "READY"}                                                                                                                                              |

sections.heading: Disk States
sections.body:
The following table describes the possible `diskState` values in your
pre-defined replica set tags.

| Disk State | Description                                                                                         |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| READY      | Only target warm nodes.You can run queries without experiencing increased or unpredictable latency. |

For an example of this replica set tag, see
[Reduce Secondary Disk Warming Impact](reduce-secondary-disk-warming-example).

sections.heading: Node Types
sections.body:
The following table describes the possible `nodeType` values in your
pre-defined replica set tags.

| Node Type  | Description                                                                                                                                     |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| ELECTABLE  | Read from nodes eligible to be elected primary. ELECTABLE nodes correspond to Electable nodes for high availability in the cluster creation UI. |
| READ\_ONLY | Read from read-only nodes. READ\_ONLY nodes correspond to Read-only nodes for optimal local reads in the cluster creation UI.                   |
| ANALYTICS  | Read from read-only analytics nodes. ANALYTICS nodes correspond to Analytics nodes for workload isolation in the cluster creation UI.           |

To learn how to configure electable, read-only, and analytics nodes for
your cluster, see [Configure High Availability and Workload Isolation](create-cluster-multi-region).

> **See Also:** For details on how these pre-defined replica set tags correspond to
> BI Connector for Atlas read preferences, refer to the BI Connector cluster options
> section of the [Create a Cluster Page](bic-read-preferences).

sections.heading: Use Cases and Examples
sections.body:
Consider the following scenarios where utilizing pre-defined replica set
tags would be beneficial, and see the corresponding sample connection
strings.

> **Important:** 
>
> High Availability for Analytics Node Queries
>
> For reads targeting analytic nodes with tags like `readPreferenceTags=nodeType:ANALYTICS`,
> resilience is crucial when node availability fluctuates. Without fallback tags,
> queries may fail during:
>
> - Initial sync during data compaction
>
> - Disk scale-down events
>
> - Any NVMe (non-volatile memory express) cluster tier changes
>
> To mitigate these risks, ensure your connection string includes *multiple read
> preference tags* to provide fallback options so Atlas can route analytic
> reads to available nodes during such events. For example:
>
> ```none
> db+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@foo-q8x1v.mycluster.com/test?readPreference=secondary&readPreferenceTags=nodeType:ANALYTICS,priority:1&readPreferenceTags=&readConcernLevel=local
> ```
>
> In this example:
>
> - The application first tries to connect to a node tagged `nodeType:ANALYTICS`.
>
> - If no such nodes are available, the final empty `readPreferenceTags=`
>   allows the application to connect to any available secondary node.

> **Note:** Each of the following example connection strings employ the
> `readConcernLevel=local` connection string option. Specifying
> a [read concern of local](manual/reference/read-concern-local)
> ensures that secondary reads on sharded clusters do not
> return [orphaned documents](manual/reference/glossary). You do not
> need to specify this option when connecting to non-sharded replica sets.

sections.heading: Use Analytics Nodes to Isolate Workloads
sections.body:
If an application performs complex or long-running operations, such as
ETL (Extract Transform Load) or reporting, you may want to
isolate the application's queries from the rest of your operational
workload by connecting exclusively to analytics nodes.

Consider the following connection string:

```none
mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@foo-q8x1v.mycluster.com/test?readPreference=secondary&readPreferenceTags=nodeType:ANALYTICS&readConcernLevel=local
```

The connection string options appear in the following order:

- `readPreference=secondary`

- `readPreferenceTags=nodeType:ANALYTICS`

- `readConcernLevel=local`

The [readPreference](manual/reference/read-preference) option of
`secondary` and [readPreferenceTag](manual/core/read-preference) option of
`{ nodeType : ANALYTICS }` limit the application connections to
analytic nodes.

sections.heading: Isolate Normal Application Secondary Reads from Analytics Nodes
sections.body:
You may want to isolate regular application reads from the workload on
analytics nodes.

Consider the following connection string:

```none
mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@foo-q8x1v.mycluster.com/test?readPreference=secondary&readPreferenceTags=workloadType:OPERATIONAL&readConcernLevel=local
```

The connection string options appear in the following order:

- `readPreference=secondary`

- `readPreferenceTags=workloadType:OPERATIONAL`

- `readConcernLevel=local`

The specified options prevent your application from reading from
analytics nodes. The application must read from `operational`, or
non-analytics, nodes.

sections.heading: Target Local Reads for Geographically-Distributed Applications
sections.body:
Use pre-defined replica set tags to target local reads to
specific regions for globally distributed applications. Prior to the
introduction of these pre-defined replica set tags, local reads for
globally distributed applications relied on correctly calculating the
[nearest](manual/reference/read-preference) read
preference. With pre-defined replica set tags, specifying appropriate
geographic tags in combination with a read preference mode of
`nearest` provides more consistent behavior.

The following connection string prioritizes connections to the
AWS (Amazon Web Services) `US_EAST_1` region, followed by the `US_EAST_2` region:

```none
mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@foo-q8x1v.mycluster.com/test?readPreference=nearest&readPreferenceTags=provider:AWS,region:US_EAST_1&readPreferenceTags=provider:AWS,region:US_EAST_2&readPreferenceTags=&readConcernLevel=local
```

The connection string options appear in the following order:

- `readPreference=nearest`

- `readPreferenceTags=provider:AWS,region:US_EAST_1`

- `readPreferenceTags=provider:AWS,region:US_EAST_2`

- `readPreferenceTags=`

- `readConcernLevel=local`

Atlas considers each read preference tag in the order you specify
them. After Atlas matches a node to a tag, it finds all eligible
nodes that match that tag. Atlas then ignores any following
`readPreferenceTags`.

In this example, the application first tries to connect to a node in
AWS (Amazon Web Services) region `US_EAST_1`. If there no nodes in that region are
available, the application tries to connect to a node in AWS (Amazon Web Services) region
`US_EAST_2`.

The final (empty) `readPreferenceTags=` provides a fallback option.
With an empty `readPreferenceTags=` option, the application can
connect to any available node regardless of provider or region.

These options help ensure that the application connects to the closest
geographic region for reduced latency and improved performance.

> **Note:** You can further target reads based on availability zones.

> **See Also:** For additional information and use cases for various read
> preferences, refer to the
> [Read Preference page in the MongoDB Manual](manual/reference/read-preference).

sections.heading: Reduce Secondary Disk Warming Impact
sections.body:
When Atlas adds or replaces a node in your cluster, it performs
[disk pre-warming](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ebs-initialize.html)
by default. During the disk pre-warming process, the newly created storage
volume undergoes a period of heavy IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) usage. This slows down read operations
made against this node. Therefore, during the disk pre-warming process,
Atlas keeps the pre-warming node hidden by default, preventing it
from participating in any read operations.

If you prefer that a newly added or replaced node becomes immediately active
and visible, you can choose to [Enable or Disable Fast Disk Pre-Warming](disk-pre-warming)
and prevent read operations on a warming node by using a connection string
as in the following example:

```none
mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@foo-q8x1v.mycluster.com/test?readPreference=secondary&readPreferenceTags=diskState:READY&readConcernLevel=local
```

The connection string options appear in the following order:

- `readPreference=secondary`

- `readPreferenceTags=diskState:READY`

- `readConcernLevel=local`

The [readPreference](manual/reference/read-preference) option of
`secondary` and [readPreferenceTag](manual/core/read-preference) option of `{ diskState : READY }`
tells Atlas to only target warm nodes.

sections.heading: Retrieve Availability Zones
sections.body:
Atlas provides pre-defined replica set tags for availability
zones by default. These tags include the AWS (Amazon Web Services) availability zone ID,
Google Cloud fully-qualified name for a zone, or Azure (Microsoft Azure) zone number. You can
check the availability zone of a node by using the [rs.conf()](manual/reference/method/rs.conf) shell method or by viewing the last ping of the cluster
node.

> **Example:** 
>
> ```sh
> ...
>
> "tags": {
>           "availabilityZone": "use2-az2",
>
> ...
> ```

sections.heading: Built-In Custom Write Concerns
sections.body:
Atlas provides built-in custom write concerns for multi-region
clusters. Write concern describes the level of acknowledgment requested
from MongoDB for write operations to a cluster.

Atlas's built-in custom write concerns can help improve
data consistency by ensuring your operations are propagated to a set
number of regions to succeed.

To use a custom write concern, specify the
write concern in the [write concern document](manual/reference/write-concern) of your operation.

Atlas provides the following custom write concerns for multi-region
clusters:

| Write Concern | Tags             | Description                                                                                                  |
| ------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| twoRegions    | \{ region: 2 }   | Write operations must be acknowledged by at least two regions in your cluster.                               |
| threeRegions  | \{ region: 3 }   | Write operations must be acknowledged by at least three regions in your cluster.                             |
| twoProviders  | \{ provider: 2 } | Write operations must be acknowledged by at least two regions in your cluster with distinct cloud providers. |

> **Example:** Consider a multi-region cluster across three regions:
> **us-east-1**, **us-east-2**, and **us-west-1**. You want to have
> write operations propagate to all three regions in your cluster
> before Atlas accepts them.
>
> The following operation inserts a document and requires that the
> operation be propagated to all three regions due to the
> `{ w: "threeRegions" }` write concern object:
>
> ```javascript
> db.employees.insertOne(
>   { name: "Bob Smith", company: "MongoDB" },
>   { writeConcern: { w: "threeRegions", j: true } }
> )
> ```
